title: gastrodia molloyi
intro: Gastrodia molloyi is a rare and endangered orchid species native to Western Australia, characterized by its unique, white or yellowish-green flowers and cylindrical, red-brown seed pods. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Gastrodia molloyi include its potential to treat various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. Gastrodia molloyi is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, and it is also used in various herbal preparations, including ointments, creams, and salves.
